mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts show no statistical significance above baseline (p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling period, or forward quarterly returns are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects strengthening economic output without triggering monetary tightening, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of observations during stable inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate expanding employment and rising aggregate income, which supports corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.5 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0 percent, markets price in aggressive future monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 50% of periods where inflation exceeds 3.5 percent and rates stay below 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms robust consumer demand driving economic expansion, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales growth signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production during a period of rising interest rates reflects demand destruction from monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth coincides with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 65% of observations during tightening cycles.
